fields.why: Runtime targeting already has the exclusion array; the template now names its structured source.

log.why: Creature-type exclusions are a reusable target-filter mechanic.

fields.comment: Runtime targeting already has the exclusion array; the template now names its structured source. It maps to `targeting.filter.excludeCreatureTypes[]`. It is populated from the structured markdown label `Target Excluded Creature Types`. Purpose: targeting.filter.excludeCreatureTypes[] exists so canonical prose, structured markdown, and runtime JSON do not silently disagree about this mechanic. Populate it from the structured markdown field `Target Excluded Creature Types`, after that field has been checked against canonical prose. It maps to runtime JSON path `targeting.filter.excludeCreatureTypes[]`. The value type is `string-array`. This is optional or conditional in the runtime shape. Omit or leave empty only when this runtime/template layer intentionally treats the field as optional.

fields.why: Runtime targeting already has the size array; the template now names its structured source.

log.why: Size filters are a reusable target eligibility mechanic.

Populate it from the structured markdown field `Target Filter Sizes`, after that field has been checked against canonical prose. It maps to runtime JSON path `targeting.filter.sizes[]`. The value type is `string-array`. This is optional or conditional in the runtime shape. Omit or leave empty only when this runtime/template layer intentionally treats the field as optional.
